transport.c(3) Library Functions Manual transport.c(3)

NAME

transport.c - WebSocket transport implementation for ACIP protocol.

SYNOPSIS

Macros


#define WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_INCOMING 64
Maximum incoming message queue size (frames received from peer). #define WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_OUTGOING 512
Maximum outgoing message queue size (frames to send to peer). #define WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_INCOMING
#define WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_OUTGOING

Functions


acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_client_transport_create (const char *name, const char *url, crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx)
Create WebSocket client transport. acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_server_transport_create (const char *name, struct lws *wsi, crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx)
Create WebSocket server transport from existing connection.

Detailed Description

WebSocket transport implementation for ACIP protocol.

Implements the acip_transport_t interface for WebSocket connections. Enables browser clients to connect via WebSocket protocol.

ARCHITECTURE:

  • Uses libwebsockets for WebSocket protocol handling
  • Async libwebsockets callbacks bridge to sync recv() via ringbuffer
  • Thread-safe receive queue handles async message arrival
  • Same pattern as WebRTC transport for consistency

MESSAGE FLOW:

1.
send(): Synchronous write via lws_write()
2.
LWS callback: Async write to receive ringbuffer
3.
recv(): Blocking read from receive ringbuffer

MEMORY OWNERSHIP:

  • Transport OWNS wsi (WebSocket instance)
  • Receive queue owns buffered message data
  • recv() allocates message buffer, caller must free

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

February 2026

Definition in file websocket/transport.c.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_INCOMING 64

Maximum incoming message queue size (frames received from peer). Set to 64 for balance: allows buffering across one large frame's fragments while keeping latency reasonable. With ~70 fragments per 291KB frame, we need at least 64 slots to avoid dropping the frame while it's fragmenting.

Definition at line 57 of file websocket/transport.c.

#define WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_OUTGOING 512

Maximum outgoing message queue size (frames to send to peer). Set to 64 to match incoming queue and prevent "BINARY after FIN" errors. Large video frames fragment into ~70 chunks; queue must be large enough to buffer one complete message worth of fragments.

Definition at line 66 of file websocket/transport.c.

#define WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_INCOMING

Definition at line 69 of file websocket/transport.c.

Referenced by acip_websocket_client_transport_create(), and acip_websocket_server_transport_create().

#define WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_OUTGOING

Definition at line 70 of file websocket/transport.c.

Referenced by acip_websocket_client_transport_create(), and acip_websocket_server_transport_create().

Function Documentation

acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_client_transport_create (const char * name, const char * url, crypto_context_t * crypto_ctx)

Create WebSocket client transport.

Parameters

url WebSocket URL (e.g., "ws://localhost:27225")
crypto_ctx Optional encryption context (can be NULL)

Returns

Transport instance or NULL on failure

Definition at line 1305 of file websocket/transport.c.

1306                                                                                        {
1307   if (!name) {
1308     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Transport name is required");
1309     return NULL;
1310   }
1311 
1312   if (!url) {
1313     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "url is required");
1314     return NULL;
1315   }
1316 
1317   // Parse URL to extract host, port, and path
1318   // Format: ws://host:port/path or wss://host:port/path
1319   const char *protocol_end = strstr(url, "://");
1320   if (!protocol_end) {
1321     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid WebSocket URL format (missing ://)");
1322     return NULL;
1323   }
1324 
1325   bool use_ssl = (strncmp(url, "wss://", 6) == 0);
1326   const char *host_start = protocol_end + 3;
1327 
1328   // Find port (if specified)
1329   const char *port_start = strchr(host_start, ':');
1330   const char *path_start = strchr(host_start, '/');
1331 
1332   char host[256] = {0};
1333   int port = use_ssl ? 443 : 27226; // Default: wss:// uses 443, ws:// uses 27226 (ascii-chat WebSocket port)
1334   char path[256] = "/";
1335 
1336   if (port_start && (!path_start || port_start < path_start)) {
1337     // Port is specified
1338     size_t host_len = port_start - host_start;
1339     if (host_len >= sizeof(host)) {
1340       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Host name too long");
1341       return NULL;
1342     }
1343     memcpy(host, host_start, host_len);
1344     host[host_len] = '\0';
1345 
1346     // Extract port
1347     port = atoi(port_start + 1);
1348     if (port <= 0 || port > 65535) {
1349       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid port number");
1350       return NULL;
1351     }
1352   } else {
1353     // No port specified, use default
1354     size_t host_len = path_start ? (size_t)(path_start - host_start) : strlen(host_start);
1355     if (host_len >= sizeof(host)) {
1356       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Host name too long");
1357       return NULL;
1358     }
1359     memcpy(host, host_start, host_len);
1360     host[host_len] = '\0';
1361   }
1362 
1363   // Extract path
1364   if (path_start) {
1365     SAFE_STRNCPY(path, path_start, sizeof(path) - 1);
1366     path[sizeof(path) - 1] = '\0';
1367   }
1368 
1369   log_info("Connecting to WebSocket: %s (host=%s, port=%d, path=%s, ssl=%d)", url, host, port, path, use_ssl);
1370 
1371   // Allocate transport structure
1372   acip_transport_t *transport = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(acip_transport_t), acip_transport_t *);
1373   if (!transport) {
1374     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport");
1375     return NULL;
1376   }
1377 
1378   // Allocate WebSocket-specific data
1379   websocket_transport_data_t *ws_data =
1380       SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(websocket_transport_data_t), websocket_transport_data_t *);
1381   if (!ws_data) {
1382     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1383     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport data");
1384     return NULL;
1385   }
1386 
1387   // Initialize destruction state flag (CALLOC zero-initializes, but explicit for clarity)
1388   atomic_store_bool(&ws_data->is_destroying, false);
1389 
1390   // Create receive queue
1391   ws_data->recv_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_recv_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE);
1392   if (!ws_data->recv_queue) {
1393     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1394     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1395     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create receive queue");
1396     return NULL;
1397   }
1398 
1399   // Create send queue for client transport to buffer outgoing messages
1400   ws_data->send_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE);
1401   if (!ws_data->send_queue) {
1402     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1403     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1404     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1405     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create send queue");
1406     return NULL;
1407   }
1408 
1409   // Initialize synchronization primitives with transport-aware names
1410   char recv_name[64], state_name[64];
1411   snprintf(recv_name, sizeof(recv_name), "recv_%s", name);
1412   snprintf(state_name, sizeof(state_name), "state_%s", name);
1413 
1414   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->recv_mutex, recv_name) != 0) {
1415     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1416     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1417     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1418     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1419     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize recv mutex");
1420     return NULL;
1421   }
1422 
1423   if (cond_init(&ws_data->recv_cond, recv_name) != 0) {
1424     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1425     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1426     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1427     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1428     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1429     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize recv condition variable");
1430     return NULL;
1431   }
1432 
1433   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->state_mutex, state_name) != 0) {
1434     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1435     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1436     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1437     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1438     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1439     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1440     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize state mutex");
1441     return NULL;
1442   }
1443 
1444   if (cond_init(&ws_data->state_cond, state_name) != 0) {
1445     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1446     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1447     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1448     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1449     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1450     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1451     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1452     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize state condition variable");
1453     return NULL;
1454   }
1455 
1456   // Allocate initial send buffer
1457   ws_data->send_buffer_capacity = LWS_PRE + 524288; // Match tx_packet_size (512KB)
1458   ws_data->send_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(ws_data->send_buffer_capacity, uint8_t *);
1459   if (!ws_data->send_buffer) {
1460     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1461     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1462     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1463     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1464     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1465     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1466     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1467     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate send buffer");
1468     return NULL;
1469   }
1470 
1471   // Create libwebsockets context
1472   // Protocol array must persist for lifetime of context - use static
1473   // IMPORTANT: "http" protocol MUST be first for WebSocket upgrade handshake to work
1474   static struct lws_protocols client_protocols[] = {
1475       {
1476           "http", // Required first for WebSocket upgrade
1477           websocket_callback,
1478           0,      // Per-session data (unused, using connect_info.userdata instead)
1479           524288, // RX buffer size
1480           0,      // ID
1481           NULL,   // User pointer (will be set from connect_info.userdata)
1482           524288  // TX packet size
1483       },
1484       {
1485           "acip", // ACIP protocol
1486           websocket_callback,
1487           0,      // Per-session data (unused, using connect_info.userdata instead)
1488           524288, // RX buffer size
1489           0,      // ID
1490           NULL,   // User pointer (will be set from connect_info.userdata)
1491           524288  // TX packet size
1492       },
1493       {NULL, NULL, 0, 0, 0, NULL, 0} // Terminator
1494   };
1495 
1496   // Disable client compression for now - causes assertion in lws_set_extension_option()
1497   // This is a known issue with libwebsockets permessage-deflate negotiation
1498   // Server-side compression is still enabled, data will be compressed from server to client
1499   // but client->server traffic remains uncompressed (acceptable since client sends less data)
1500   // static const struct lws_extension client_extensions[] = {
1501   //     {"permessage-deflate", lws_extension_callback_pm_deflate, "permessage-deflate; client_max_window_bits=15"},
1502   //     {NULL, NULL, NULL}};
1503 
1504   struct lws_context_creation_info info;
1505   memset(&info, 0, sizeof(info));
1506   info.port = CONTEXT_PORT_NO_LISTEN; // Client mode - no listening
1507   info.protocols = client_protocols;
1508   info.gid = (gid_t)-1; // Cast to avoid undefined behavior with unsigned type
1509   info.uid = (uid_t)-1; // Cast to avoid undefined behavior with unsigned type
1510   // Initialize SSL globally for client (required even for clients to use OpenSSL)
1511   // The LWS_SERVER_OPTION_DO_SSL_GLOBAL_INIT flag works for both servers and clients
1512   info.options = LWS_SERVER_OPTION_DO_SSL_GLOBAL_INIT;
1513   info.extensions = NULL; // Disable client compression due to lws_set_extension_option() assertion
1514 
1515   // Increase per-thread service buffer to prevent fragmentation of large messages
1516   // Default is 4KB, causing 291KB frames to fragment into 73 × 4KB chunks
1517   // Increase to 512KB to match server and allow larger WebSocket frames without fragmentation
1518   info.pt_serv_buf_size = 512 * 1024; // 512KB per-thread service buffer
1519 
1520   // Configure keep-alive to prevent idle disconnects during handshake
1521   // PING every 30 seconds if idle, close after 35 seconds total with no response
1522   static const lws_retry_bo_t client_keep_alive_policy = {
1523       .secs_since_valid_ping = 30,   // Send PING after 30s idle
1524       .secs_since_valid_hangup = 35, // Hangup if still idle after 35s
1525   };
1526   info.retry_and_idle_policy = &client_keep_alive_policy;
1527 
1528   // Enable libwebsockets logging through centralized logging system
1529   lws_log_init_client();
1530 
1531   ws_data->context = lws_create_context(&info);
1532 
1533   if (!ws_data->context) {
1534     SAFE_FREE(ws_data->send_buffer);
1535     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1536     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1537     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1538     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1539     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1540     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1541     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1542     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1543     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to create libwebsockets context");
1544     return NULL;
1545   }
1546 
1547   // Connect to WebSocket server
1548   log_debug("Initiating WebSocket connection to %s:%d%s", host, port, path);
1549   struct lws_client_connect_info connect_info;
1550   memset(&connect_info, 0, sizeof(connect_info));
1551   connect_info.context = ws_data->context;
1552   connect_info.address = host;
1553   connect_info.port = port;
1554   connect_info.path = path;
1555   connect_info.host = host;
1556   connect_info.origin = host;
1557   connect_info.protocol = "acip";
1558   // Use SSL + skip server certificate hostname verification + allow self-signed certs (for development)
1559   connect_info.ssl_connection = use_ssl ? (LCCSCF_USE_SSL | LCCSCF_SKIP_SERVER_CERT_HOSTNAME_CHECK | LCCSCF_ALLOW_SELFSIGNED) : 0;
1560   connect_info.userdata = ws_data;
1561 
1562   log_debug("Calling lws_client_connect_via_info...");
1563   ws_data->wsi = lws_client_connect_via_info(&connect_info);
1564   log_debug("lws_client_connect_via_info returned: %p", (void *)ws_data->wsi);
1565   if (!ws_data->wsi) {
1566     lws_context_destroy(ws_data->context);
1567     SAFE_FREE(ws_data->send_buffer);
1568     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1569     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1570     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1571     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1572     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1573     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1574     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1575     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1576     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to connect to WebSocket server");
1577     return NULL;
1578   }
1579 
1580   ws_data->is_connected = false;      // Will be set to true in LWS_CALLBACK_CLIENT_ESTABLISHED
1581   ws_data->connection_failed = false; // Set to true in LWS_CALLBACK_CLIENT_CONNECTION_ERROR
1582   ws_data->owns_context = true;       // Client transport owns the context
1583 
1584   // Initialize transport
1585   transport->methods = &websocket_methods;
1586   transport->crypto_ctx = crypto_ctx;
1587   transport->impl_data = ws_data;
1588 
1589   // CRITICAL: Give libwebsockets time to initialize connection before starting service thread
1590   // libwebsockets needs to process the initial connection handshake callbacks before lws_service()
1591   // is called repeatedly. Concurrent access to context during connection setup can trigger assertions.
1592   // Sleep 50ms to let lws_client_connect_via_info() callbacks complete initialization.
1593   log_debug("Delaying service thread start to allow libwebsockets connection initialization...");
1594   platform_sleep_us(50000); // 50ms delay
1595 
1596   // Start service thread after connection initialization
1597   // Only the service thread should call lws_service() on this context
1598   log_debug("Starting WebSocket service thread...");
1599   ws_data->service_running = true;
1600   if (asciichat_thread_create(&ws_data->service_thread, "ws_service", websocket_service_thread, ws_data) != 0) {
1601     log_error("Failed to create WebSocket service thread");
1602     ws_data->service_running = false;
1603     lws_context_destroy(ws_data->context);
1604     SAFE_FREE(ws_data->send_buffer);
1605     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1606     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1607     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1608     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1609     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1610     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1611     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1612     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1613     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to create service thread");
1614     return NULL;
1615   }
1616   log_debug("WebSocket service thread started");
1617 
1618   // CRITICAL FIX: Do NOT block the main thread waiting for connection!
1619   // This prevents stdin/keyboard input from being processed and causes the client to hang.
1620   // The service thread will establish the connection asynchronously.
1621   // The protocol layer (recv) will detect if connection fails and handle it there.
1622   // Return immediately so the main thread can respond to keyboard input.
1623   log_debug("WebSocket transport created, service thread will establish connection asynchronously");
1624 
1625   // Register transport
1626   NAMED_REGISTER_TRANSPORT(transport, name, NULL);
1627 
1628   // Register websocket implementation data with transport as parent
1629   NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET_IMPL(ws_data, "impl", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(transport));
1630 
1631   // Return transport immediately - connection will be established by service thread
1632   // If connection fails, recv() will detect is_connected=false and return error
1633   return transport;
1634 }




References asciichat_thread_create(),
    atomic_store_bool(), lws_log_init_client(),
    mutex_destroy(), mutex_init(), platform_sleep_us(),
    ringbuffer_create(), ringbuffer_destroy(),
    WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE, and WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE.


Referenced by server_connection_establish(), and
    websocket_client_connect().






acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_server_transport_create
  (const char * name, struct lws * wsi, crypto_context_t * crypto_ctx)


Create WebSocket server transport from existing connection. Wraps
    an already-established libwebsockets connection (from server accept). Used
    by websocket_server module to create transports for incoming clients.




Parameters


wsi Established libwebsockets connection (not
  owned by transport)


crypto_ctx Optional crypto context


Returns


Transport instance or NULL on error


Definition at line 1646 of file
    websocket/transport.c.


1647                                                                                        {
1648   if (!name) {
1649     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Transport name is required");
1650     return NULL;
1651   }
1652 
1653   if (!wsi) {
1654     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid wsi parameter");
1655     return NULL;
1656   }
1657 
1658   // Allocate transport structure
1659   acip_transport_t *transport = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(acip_transport_t), acip_transport_t *);
1660   if (!transport) {
1661     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport");
1662     return NULL;
1663   }
1664 
1665   // Allocate transport-specific data
1666   websocket_transport_data_t *ws_data =
1667       SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(websocket_transport_data_t), websocket_transport_data_t *);
1668   if (!ws_data) {
1669     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1670     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport data");
1671     return NULL;
1672   }
1673 
1674   // Initialize receive queue
1675   ws_data->recv_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_recv_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE);
1676   if (!ws_data->recv_queue) {
1677     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1678     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1679     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create receive queue");
1680     return NULL;
1681   }
1682 
1683   // Initialize send queue (for server-side transports)
1684   ws_data->send_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_recv_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE);
1685   if (!ws_data->send_queue) {
1686     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1687     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1688     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1689     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create send queue");
1690     return NULL;
1691   }
1692 
1693   // Initialize pending-free queue for deferred buffer freeing
1694   // permessage-deflate compression holds buffer references asynchronously,
1695   // so we defer freeing to prevent use-after-free errors
1696   ws_data->pending_free_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(pending_free_item_t), 256);
1697   if (!ws_data->pending_free_queue) {
1698     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1699     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1700     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1701     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1702     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create pending-free queue");
1703     return NULL;
1704   }
1705 
1706   // Initialize synchronization primitives with transport-aware names
1707   char recv_name[64], send_name[64], state_name[64], pending_free_name[64];
1708   snprintf(recv_name, sizeof(recv_name), "recv_%s", name);
1709   snprintf(send_name, sizeof(send_name), "send_%s", name);
1710   snprintf(state_name, sizeof(state_name), "state_%s", name);
1711   snprintf(pending_free_name, sizeof(pending_free_name), "pending_free_%s", name);
1712 
1713   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->recv_mutex, recv_name) != 0) {
1714     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1715     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1716     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1717     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1718     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize recv mutex");
1719     return NULL;
1720   }
1721 
1722   if (cond_init(&ws_data->recv_cond, recv_name) != 0) {
1723     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1724     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1725     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1726     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1727     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1728     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize recv condition variable");
1729     return NULL;
1730   }
1731 
1732   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->send_mutex, send_name) != 0) {
1733     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1734     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1735     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1736     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1737     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1738     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1739     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize send mutex");
1740     return NULL;
1741   }
1742 
1743   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->state_mutex, state_name) != 0) {
1744     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1745     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1746     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1747     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1748     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1749     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1750     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1751     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize state mutex");
1752     return NULL;
1753   }
1754 
1755   if (cond_init(&ws_data->state_cond, state_name) != 0) {
1756     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1757     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1758     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1759     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1760     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1761     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1762     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1763     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1764     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize state condition variable");
1765     return NULL;
1766   }
1767 
1768   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->pending_free_mutex, pending_free_name) != 0) {
1769     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1770     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1771     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1772     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1773     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1774     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1775     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1776     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->pending_free_queue);
1777     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1778     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1779     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize pending-free mutex");
1780     return NULL;
1781   }
1782 
1783   // Allocate send buffer with LWS_PRE padding
1784   size_t initial_capacity = 4096 + LWS_PRE;
1785   ws_data->send_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(initial_capacity, uint8_t *);
1786   if (!ws_data->send_buffer) {
1787     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1788     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1789     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1790     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1791     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1792     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1793     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1794     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1795     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate send buffer");
1796     return NULL;
1797   }
1798   ws_data->send_buffer_capacity = initial_capacity;
1799 
1800   // Store connection info (server-side: no context ownership, connection already established)
1801   ws_data->wsi = wsi;
1802   ws_data->context = lws_get_context(wsi); // Get context from wsi (not owned)
1803   ws_data->owns_context = false;           // Server owns context, not transport
1804   ws_data->is_connected = true;            // Already connected (server-side)
1805   log_info("[WEBSOCKET_TRANSPORT_CREATE] ★★★ SERVER TRANSPORT CREATED: is_connected=true, wsi=%p, ws_data=%p",
1806            (void *)wsi, (void *)ws_data);
1807   log_debug("Server transport created: is_connected=true, wsi=%p", (void *)wsi);
1808 
1809   // Initialize transport
1810   transport->methods = &websocket_methods;
1811   transport->crypto_ctx = crypto_ctx;
1812   transport->impl_data = ws_data;
1813 
1814   log_info("Created WebSocket server transport (crypto: %s)", crypto_ctx ? "enabled" : "disabled");
1815 
1816   // Register transport
1817   NAMED_REGISTER_TRANSPORT(transport, name, NULL);
1818 
1819   // Register websocket implementation data with transport as parent
1820   NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET_IMPL(ws_data, "impl", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(transport));
1821 
1822   return transport;
1823 }






References mutex_destroy(), mutex_init(),
    ringbuffer_create(), ringbuffer_destroy(),
    WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE, and WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE.








Author


Generated automatically by Doxygen for ascii-chat from the source
    code.







  

    Version 0.9.0
    ascii-chat
